About this site

Colophon

Tech Stack

Built with Astro for static-first delivery with selective React hydration for interactive components. 3D hero animation rendered with Three.js. Animations powered by GSAP (ScrollTrigger, Draggable, SplitText). Smooth scrolling via Lenis. Deployed on Vercel.

Typography

Playfair Display is used for display and headline roles, paired with DM Sans for body text, titles, labels, and UI elements. The serif/sans pairing creates a two-register type system — editorial for impact, geometric sans for readability. The 5×3 typescale grid (display, headline, title, body, label × lg/md/sm) follows M3 conventions.

Design Language

The prismatic/CMY (cyan, magenta, yellow) chromatic separation is used throughout as a unifying visual language. It appears in scroll reveals, hover effects, the hero animation's temporal RGB split, and the carousel's velocity-driven shadows. This references how white light splits through a prism — a metaphor for breaking a design system into its constituent parts.

Accessibility

This site respects prefers-reduced-motion — all animations are disabled or minimized when the user has requested reduced motion. Navigation is fully keyboard-accessible, the about modal includes a focus trap, and all images include alt text.

Sam Stringer-Hye

© 2026

Colophon

Sam Stringer-Hye

Product designer at Razorfish with 10+ years across UI, UX, motion, and design systems. I've shipped work for Samsung, Frontier, and CVS. Mostly I obsess over the details most people scroll right past.

I care a lot about craft. How a transition feels, whether the type hierarchy actually works, if a component holds up at every breakpoint. That stuff never shows up in a brief, but you always notice it in the final product.
